common.tagline: Your rights when government says no

home.heroEyebrow: Understand · Act in time · Get help
home.heroTitle: Got a government ‘no’? Let’s work out what you can do.
home.heroLead: We explain the letter in plain English, find the time limit that applies, draft your first letter, and route you to free help — grounded in sourced rules.
home.ctaStart: Find out what you can do
home.howStep1Body: Pick the kind of decision, or paste the letter. A few plain questions — no logins.
home.howStep2Body: The rule that applies, with the official sources we used.
home.howStep3Body: Edit a ready-made draft, or get routed to free help that can take it from here.
home.deadlineRule: Reviews usually have a strict time limit.
home.deadlineSub: We show the rule that applies and link the official source — checked and dated.
home.trust2Body: Every answer shows where it’s from.
home.trust4Body: Real people, when you need them.
home.helpBody: Free help is one tap away — government, tribunals, and community legal centres.
home.helpCta: See the three tiers of help

wizard.title: Let's work out what you can do
wizard.intro: Answer a couple of quick questions. We'll explain your options in plain English, find the time limit, and point you to free help. We never keep your details.
wizard.step1Help: Pick the closest one. If none fit, you can paste your letter instead.
wizard.pasteOption: None of these — let me paste my letter
wizard.pasteHelp: Paste the words from your letter. We read it on the spot to work out which guide fits, then discard it.
wizard.pastePlaceholder: Paste what the letter says here…
wizard.step2Help: This is usually the date printed on your letter, or the day you received it. We use it to work out any time limit. You can skip this if you're not sure.
wizard.contextPlaceholder: A short note in your own words. Don't include anything you'd rather not write down.
wizard.fallbackNote: We matched this to a general guide. The details of your situation matter, so it's worth checking with a free service.
wizard.notCoveredTitle: We don't have a guide for this yet
wizard.notCoveredBody: We couldn't match this to confirmed Victorian information, so we won't guess. A free legal service can explain your options.

ask.title: Ask a question
ask.intro: Ask about a government decision in your own words. We answer in plain language and show where the answer comes from.
ask.placeholder: For example: Centrelink says I owe a debt — can I ask them to look at it again?
ask.notCoveredTitle: We're not sure about this one
ask.notCoveredBody: We don't have confirmed information that covers your question, so we won't guess. A free legal service can help with your situation.
ask.disclaimerNote: This is general information, not advice about your case.

decode.title: Scan or paste a letter
decode.intro: Take a photo of the letter, upload it, or paste the text. We read it on the spot to explain it — and then we discard it. We never store your letter.
decode.uploadLabel: Photo or PDF of the letter
decode.pasteLabel: Or paste the text of the letter
decode.pastePlaceholder: Paste what the letter says here…
decode.ocrUnavailableBody: Reading photos isn't available right now. You can type or paste the words from your letter into the box and we'll explain them.
decode.options: What you may be able to do
decode.fallbackNote: We matched this to a general guide. The details of your situation matter, so it's worth checking with a free service.
decode.notCoveredTitle: We don't have a guide for this yet
decode.notCoveredBody: We couldn't match this letter to confirmed information, so we won't guess. A free legal service can explain it and your options.

deadline.title: The important date
deadline.decisionDateLabel: What date is on the decision letter?
deadline.renderableLead: Based on the date you gave, the time limit for this step is
deadline.passedWarning: This date may have already passed. A time limit being missed doesn't always end things — a free service can tell you what's still possible.
deadline.addReminder: Add a reminder to my calendar
deadline.reminderDefault: Time limit to act on a government decision

draft.title: A draft you can use
draft.intro: Here's a starting letter you can edit and send yourself. It's a scaffold, not a finished document — change anything that doesn't fit, and fill in the parts in brackets.
draft.contextLabel: Anything you'd like the letter to mention (optional)
draft.contextPlaceholder: A short note in your own words. Don't include anything you'd rather not write down.
draft.editNote: You can edit this text before you save or print it.

help.title: Get free help
help.intro: These services are free and independent. Talking to a person is often a good next step, especially for advice about your own situation.
help.forThis: Help for this kind of decision
help.privateTitle: If you want a private lawyer
help.privateIntro: These are paid services. The Law Institute of Victoria can refer you to a lawyer, and you can search online for lawyers who handle this kind of matter.
help.livLabel: Law Institute of Victoria — Find Your Lawyer Referral Service
help.searchLabel: Search online for a lawyer who handles this

faq.title: Common questions
faq.intro: Plain-language answers, grounded in the rules and checked by a person before we publish them.
faq.question: The question
faq.ctaTitle: Have a letter or a question of your own?
faq.ctaBody: Scan a letter or ask a question — free, in plain English.

privacy.title: Your privacy
privacy.intro: This service is built so that we don't keep your letter or your answers.
privacy.point1: Your letter is read on the spot to explain it, then discarded. We don't store it on our servers and we don't log what it says.
privacy.point2: There's no account and no login. Anything kept for your convenience stays on your own device, and you can clear it any time.
privacy.point3: We don't put your personal details into analytics.
privacy.point4: When you connect with a free legal service, you deal with them directly under their own privacy rules.

about.title: About this service
about.intro: This is a free service that helps people understand and respond to government decisions.
about.infoNotAdvice: This service gives general information, not legal advice, and cannot tell you what will happen in your case. For advice about your own situation, talk to a free legal service.
about.groundedBody: Every answer is grounded in a checked set of rules and points to where it comes from. When something isn't covered, the service says so and points you to a person who can help — it never guesses.
about.currencyBody: Rules and time limits change. Each entry carries the date it was last checked, and entries are reviewed on a regular cycle.

consent.message: We use privacy-friendly analytics to understand how the service is used. No personal details are collected.

errors.rateLimited: You've made a lot of requests. Please wait a little while and try again.
errors.capReached: You've reached this session's free usage limit. You can use your own key to continue.
errors.budget: We've reached today's free usage limit. Please try again tomorrow.
errors.unavailable: The service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a little while.
errors.badInput: Please check what you entered and try again.
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.contactUnavailable: Messages aren't set up yet. In the meantime, the Get help page lists free services.
errors.contactRateLimited: You've sent a few messages already. Please try again later.
errors.contactFailed: We couldn't send your message right now. Please try again in a moment.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.intro: Send us a message about the service and we'll read it. For help with your own situation, the free services on the Get help page are the fastest route.
contact.name: Your name
contact.sentTitle: Thanks — we'll get back to you.
contact.sentBody: We read every message. We never share your details.
contact.privacyNote: We use your message and email only to reply. We don't store them on this site or share them.

footer.infoNotAdvice: General information, not legal advice. We can't tell you what will happen in your case.
